reason: The justifications are well-supported by fan/critic consensus. The OotP Ministry corruption arc being cut (Imperius on Bode/Podmore, Umbridge advocacy, Fudge manipulation) is widely documented and the scorer correctly identifies this as the biggest agency loss. The Borgin & Burkes political calculation and Quidditch World Cup self-restraint scene losses are accurate book-to-film differences. One minor issue: the scorer claims Lucius's wand being disarmed by Harry's Expelliarmus in the DoM scene 'is not in the book corpus' - this is a plausible film-only addition but hard to verify precisely. The overall 71/100 score is reasonable given that Jason Isaacs's film performance preserved Lucius's personality well (hence 20/25) but the films systematically stripped his political/behind-the-scenes role across OotP and GoF, which justifies the lower narrative_role (14/25), motivations (11/25), and arc (10/25) scores. The arc score being lowest is also consistent with the widely noted fact that the film version of Lucius performs more direct action/fight scenes while losing the book's gradual political decline and family-driven arc shift.
